findings.summary: SessionTools.resolve() wraps all native + MCP tools into AI SDK tool() objects with Effect-based execute wrappers including phase events, permission checks via ctx.ask, and truncation. Semaphore(5) limits concurrent tool execution.

Processor handles LLM streaming events: tool-input-start/end create tool parts, tool-call event sets state to 'running' with parsed input, tool-result completes the tool call. Actual execution is done by AI SDK's streamText which calls the tool.execute() function. Processor only sees the result.
resolveTools() filters tools by permission before passing to streamText — denied tools are removed entirely. This is the permission enforcement point for agent-specific tool access.

ToolRegistry collects all builtin tools + custom tools from .opencode/{tool,tools}/*.{js,ts} + plugin tools. Discovery is via Glob.scanSync + dynamic import. No separate loading path for external vs native.

Build and Plan agents differ only in permission rules. Build allows all edits+question+plan_enter. Plan denies all edits except .opencode/plans/*.md, allows plan_exit. Permission ruleset is merged with user config and filtered in request.ts.
